title: W2 C Open Sets

1/16   Continuous Preferences

Theorem 1

If preferences ⪰ on X = Rn + are continuous, then for all x ∈ X, the

sets I(x), N W T (x) and NBT (x) are all closed.

Open Balls

Definition 1

The open ball with center x ∈ Rn and radius ϵ > 0 is the set Bϵ (x) in Rn defined by Bϵ (x) ≡ {y ∈ Rn : ∥y − x∥ < ϵ} .

ϵ x

Exercise 1

Show that Bϵ′ (z) is entirely contained in Bϵ (x).

7/16  Open and Closed Sets

8/16   Open Sets in Rn

Definition 2

The set U ⊆ Rn is open if for each x ∈ U there exists an ϵ > 0 such that the open ball Bϵ (x) is entirely contained in U .

9/16   Open Sets in Rn

Example 1

(a) The interval (a, b) ⊆ R is open.

(b) The half line (a, +∞) is also open.

(c) The open ball Bϵ (x) ⊆ Rn is an open set.

(d) The square (0, 1) × (0, 1) is an open subset of R2 .

10/16   Open Sets in Rn

Example 1

(a) The interval (a, b) ⊆ R is open.

(b) The half line (a, +∞) is also open.

(c) The open ball Bϵ (x) ⊆ Rn is an open set.

(d) The square (0, 1) × (0, 1) is an open subset of R2 .

(e) What about [a, b], [a, b) and (−∞, b]?

(f) What about the union or intersection of open sets?

10/16   Closed Sets in Rn

Definition 3

A set V ⊆ Rn is closed if its complement V c = {x ∈ Rn : x ∈ / V } is open.

11/16   Closed Sets in Rn

Definition 3

A set V ⊆ Rn is closed if its complement V c = {x ∈ Rn : x ∈ / V } is open.

Example 2

(a) The interval [a, b] ⊆ R is closed subset of R.

(b) The half line (−∞, b] is also closed.

(c) [a, b) is neither open nor closed.

(d) The closed ball B ϵ (x) ⊆ Rn is a closed set.

11/16   Open and Closed Sets

Proposition 1

(a) Any arbitrary union of open sets is open.

(b) Any finite intersection of open sets is open.

(c) Any arbitrary intersection of closed sets is closed.

(d) Any finite union of closed sets is closed.

12/16   Open and Closed Sets

Proposition 1

(a) Any arbitrary union of open sets is open.

(b) Any finite intersection of open sets is open.

(c) Any arbitrary intersection of closed sets is closed.

(d) Any finite union of closed sets is closed.

Note that (c) and (d) follow from (a) and (b) using De Morgan’s Laws.

We’ll prove Proposition 1 after a few examples.

12/16   Examples

Example 3

(a) Let Sk = − k1 , k1 be an open interval. Obtain ∞  T k=1 Sk .

Example 3

(a) Let Sk = − k1 , k1 be an open interval. Obtain ∞  T k=1 Sk .

(b) Let Tk = −2 + k1 , 2 − k1 be a closed interval. Obtain ∞   S k=1 Tk .

(c) The sets ∅ and Rn are open and closed.

13/16

S (a) Let I ⊆ N be an index set. We show that A = i∈I Ai is open.

14/16

S (a) Let I ⊆ N be an index set. We show that A = i∈I Ai is open. ◦ Let x ∈ A be arbitrarily chosen.

◦ Then x ∈ Ai for some i ∈ I.

14/16

S (a) Let I ⊆ N be an index set. We show that A = i∈I Ai is open. ◦ Let x ∈ A be arbitrarily chosen.

◦ Then x ∈ Ai for some i ∈ I.

◦ There exists an open ball Bϵ (x) contained in Ai .

◦ Thus, Bϵ (x) is contained in A.

14/16

(b) Let J ⊆ N be a finite index set. We show that A′ = T j∈J Aj is open.

15/16

(b) Let J ⊆ N be a finite index set. We show that A′ = T j∈J Aj is open. ′ ◦ Let x ∈ A .

◦ Then x ∈ Aj , for all j ∈ J.

◦ There is an open ball Bϵj (x) contained in Aj , for all j ∈ J.

15/16

(b) Let J ⊆ N be a finite index set. We show that A′ = T j∈J Aj is open. ′ ◦ Let x ∈ A .

◦ Then x ∈ Aj , for all j ∈ J.

◦ There is an open ball Bϵj (x) contained in Aj , for all j ∈ J.

◦ Take ϵ = min{ϵj : j ∈ J} > 0. (Here is where we need J to be finite.)

◦ The open ball Bϵ (x) is contained in Aj , for every j ∈ J.

15/16   Bounded and Compact Sets

Definition 4

A set S ⊆ Rn is said to be bounded if there exists an open ball centered at 0 that contains S.

A set C ⊆ Rn is said to be compact if it is closed and bounded.

16/16   Bounded and Compact Sets

Definition 4

A set S ⊆ Rn is said to be bounded if there exists an open ball centered at 0 that contains S.

A set C ⊆ Rn is said to be compact if it is closed and bounded.

Comments: ◦ Most textbooks on mathematical analysis define compact sets via open covers. In Euclidean spaces, this is equivalent to the definition given above.

◦ Compact sets are extremely important for optimization.

16/16
